title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · Operations Case: Change Preview Rollback
summary: Resolve a multi-layer workstation incident and hand off a safe, reproducible operating procedure to another technician. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux change preview rollback increment in a complete Linux operations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preview scope, snapshot relevant state, define stop conditions, and test rollback before risky filesystem, package, or service changes.
Use privilege only for the smallest justified operation while preserving command provenance, environment boundaries, and audit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Change Preview Rollback while producing a complete Linux operations evidence portfolio.
